content.claim: Rural secondary schools face infrastructure fragility and closure risk, with potential settlement-viability consequences if the local secondary institution closes partly due to student outflow toward selective tracks, though this evidence derives from a single interview and has not been systematically audited.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Evidence is qualitative testimony from a single respondent, not a systematic audit of rural school closures or viability
No data provided on rate of rural secondary-school closures or correlation with selective-track outflow
Settlement-viability claim is a secondary inference; primary evidence concerns teaching conditions and socioeconomic barriers in rural schools
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
